title: Heatwave grips Ludhiana, IMD warns of warm nights and gusty winds - The Tribune
search_term: heatwave (Amritsar OR Barnala OR Bathinda OR Faridkot OR "Fatehgarh Sahib" OR Fazilka OR Ferozepur OR Gurdaspur OR Hoshiarpur OR Jalandhar OR Kapurthala OR Ludhiana OR Mansa OR Moga OR Muktsar OR "Nawanshahr (Shahid Bhagat Singh Nagar)" OR Pathankot OR Patiala OR Rupnagar OR "Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar (Mohali)" OR Sangrur OR "Tarn Taran") Punjab
full_text:
Ludhiana reeled under searing conditions on Sunday as the mercury soared to a maximum of 42.2°C, while the minimum settled at 24.6°C, according to the Indian Meteorological Centre, Chandigarh.
The department has issued a heat wave warning for April 27, cautioning residents about warm nights, thunderstorms, and gusty winds at isolated places.
“Temperatures are running significantly above normal, and the persistence of warm nights will add to the discomfort,” an IMD official said, urging residents to take precautions against heat-related illnesses.
The advisory also noted that sudden gusts of wind and thunderstorm activity could occur in pockets of the district.
Across the city, the impact of the blazing sun was visible. Commuters wrapped their faces in cloth to shield themselves from the scorching glare, while groups of residents sought refuge under bougainvillea trees and shaded corners. The shimmering asphalt and the still air painted a picture of a city caught in the grip of summer’s fury.
Doctors have advised citizens to stay hydrated and avoid prolonged exposure during peak hours. “Heat waves can be dangerous, especially for the elderly, children, and those with chronic illnesses. Simple measures like drinking water frequently and wearing light cotton clothes can make a difference,” said a local physician.
With April not yet over, Ludhiana’s residents are bracing for what promises to be a long, testing summer. The IMD has urged vigilance, reminding citizens that timely precautions can help mitigate the risks posed by extreme weather.
